sentence_reviews.text: Distillation transfers behavior from a teacher to a smaller student, while pruning removes low-value weights, channels, heads, experts, or activations.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Hinton arXiv abstract lines 42-44 describes compressing knowledge from ensembles into a single easier-to-deploy model; Li arXiv abstract lines 38-41 describes pruning/compressing weights and filters identified as having small effects on output accuracy.
sentence_reviews.notes: Teacher/student distillation and weight/filter pruning are supported; heads, experts, and activations are broader than the inspected sources.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Knowledge distillation, pruning & sparsification is to connect Supervised learning, Calibration, uncertainty & risk objectives to downstream capabilities including Quantized inference & compressed deployment, Edge, on-device & neuromorphic deployment.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Hinton lines 42-43 supports model compression for deployment; Li lines 38-41 supports reduced computation/storage and inference costs; Jacob lines 41-42 supports efficient on-device quantized inference. Calibration/uncertainty and neuromorphic deployment are not directly evidenced.
sentence_reviews.notes: Compression/deployment links are supported; calibration/risk and neuromorphic parts need narrower evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Quantized inference & compressed deployment
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Hinton lines 42-43 describes compressed models easier to deploy; Li lines 38-41 describes pruning/compressing to reduce computation/storage/inference costs; Jacob lines 41-42 describes efficient integer-only inference and training to preserve accuracy after quantization.
sentence_reviews.notes: Compressed deployment is directly supported; quantized inference is adjacent rather than directly caused by distillation/pruning.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Edge, on-device & neuromorphic deployment
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Hinton lines 42-43 discusses deployment to many users and expensive large neural nets; Jacob lines 41-42 explicitly mentions mobile devices and on-device inference; no inspected source supports neuromorphic deployment.
sentence_reviews.notes: Edge/on-device deployment is supported; neuromorphic is unsupported by inspected evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: Results are sensitive to data quality, hyperparameters, compute budget, random variation, and evaluation contamination. Its direct dependencies include Supervised learning, Calibration, uncertainty & risk objectives.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Hinton and Li abstracts support training/retraining compressed models and accuracy preservation, but do not directly support the generic sensitivity or evaluation-contamination wording; calibration/risk is not directly evidenced.
sentence_reviews.notes: The sentence is mostly generic and overbroad for the inspected sources.

sentence_reviews.text: Knowledge distillation, pruning & sparsification uses Supervised learning as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Hinton lines 42-44 discusses training/compressing models on data and specialist models; Li line 41 describes retraining pruned networks to regain accuracy; supervised learning is a common but not exclusive setup.
sentence_reviews.notes: Defensible for standard distillation/pruning workflows, but not exclusive.

sentence_reviews.text: Knowledge distillation, pruning & sparsification is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family Calibration, uncertainty & risk objectives.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Hinton supports soft prediction/knowledge compression at a high level, but the inspected abstract does not explicitly address calibration, uncertainty, or risk objectives.
sentence_reviews.notes: Calibration is plausible for distillation but not adequately supported as a core dependency by inspected evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: Knowledge distillation, pruning & sparsification uses Affine transforms & dense layers as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Li arXiv abstract lines 38-40 discusses pruning weights, fully connected layers, convolutional layers, filters, feature maps, and dense matrix multiplication libraries.
sentence_reviews.notes: Dense/affine layers are common targets, but the node is broader than dense layers.

sentence_reviews.text: Knowledge distillation, pruning & sparsification uses Optimization theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Hinton lines 42-44 and Li line 41 both involve training/retraining compressed/pruned models to preserve or regain accuracy, implying optimization.
sentence_reviews.notes: Optimization is required by training/retraining, though the exact theory dependency is synthesized.

edge_reviews.notes: Supervised/retraining workflows are supported, but not every pruning or sparsification workflow is supervised; core strength is somewhat overstated.
Calibration/uncertainty may relate to distillation but is not directly established as a core optimize-for dependency.
Weights, fully connected layers, and dense matrix multiplication support a common affine/dense dependency.
Training/retraining compressed models supports optimization as a common dependency.
